clause_ref: IEEE 1584:2002 Annex F.16 simplified regression (7-term log-linear)
formula_latex: E = \frac{12.552}{D^x} \cdot \left( k_1 + k_2 \log I_{arc} + k_3 \log G + k_4 \log D + k_5 \log t_{arc} + k_6 V_{nom} + k_7 \right) \cdot \text{adj}
formula_plain_text: Empirical incident-energy model per IEEE 1584-2002 Annex F.16: log-log fit with 7 coefficients (k1..k7) per voltage class and electrode configuration. The 12.552 constant + distance exponent x is per-class. Adjustment factors (gap, distance, enclosure) multiply the result when non-standard conditions apply. NOTE — IEEE 1584-2018 §7 + Tables 1+3 introduce a different model shape (10-coef arc-current + 13-coef incident-energy with polynomial-in-V terms); see incident-energy-formula-2018.json for that anchor.

symbols.meaning: Incident energy at working distance D
Arcing current (either high or low bracket)
Arcing time (OCPD clearing time at I_arc)
Empirical coefficients per voltage class × electrode config

worked_examples.source: IEEE 1584-2002 Annex F worked example — approximate; verify with implementer's reference

implementation_notes: The 12.552 constant converts J/cm² to cal/cm² in IEEE 1584-2002 Annex F.16 (1 cal ≈ 4.184 J, so 1 J/cm² ≈ 0.239 cal/cm²; the 12.552 represents the IE-formula form per Annex F.16 equation). The distance exponent x depends on voltage class. The IEEE 1584-2018 model uses a different formulation — see incident-energy-formula-2018.json.
